phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your chain-heal all-ins beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — chain-heal all-ins edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your chain-heal all-ins beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — chain-heal all-ins edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your chain-heal all-ins beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: full chains with a stolen ult delete or out-brawl anything mid offers.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your chain-heal all-ins on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your chain-heal all-ins beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your chain-heal all-ins out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E2-chain into Q-W with passive whirls is the lane's best level-3 all-in nobody respects yet.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your chain-heal all-ins out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Hijack online — your R is whatever his best button is. The lane's power budget just became a shared account.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your chain-heal all-ins beat Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Sylas shops the enemy team: steal the fight-flipper, flank with it, and heal through the refund policy.

phases.why: Yone's double-blade spirit dives own this window — your chain-heal all-ins don't answer them on first item yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Sylas shops the enemy team: steal the fight-flipper, flank with it, and heal through the refund policy.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-poke the wave contacts — the chain detonation chips while you wait for the real kit at 3.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins: the heal-strike makes even trades dishonest — yours refunds, his doesn't.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E2-chain into Q-W with passive whirls is the lane's best level-3 all-in nobody respects yet.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Chain-trade on cooldown: shield in, stun, whirl the passive autos, heal out. The brawl is the plan.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Hijack online — your R is whatever his best button is. The lane's power budget just became a shared account.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: full chains with a stolen ult delete or out-brawl anything mid offers.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Sylas shops the enemy team: steal the fight-flipper, flank with it, and heal through the refund policy.
